content: I've been thinking about learning something new to keep my mind sharp. I'm turning 60 next month and want to challenge myself.
That's wonderful! Learning new skills is great at any age. What kind of things have you been considering?

Well, I've been a blockchain developer for about 8 years now, working on DeFi protocols. But lately I've been fascinated by home automation systems. I just installed some smart lights and a Nest thermostat at my parents' house in Portland.
That's impressive! Home automation can be quite technical. Are you looking to expand your knowledge there, or explore something completely different?

Actually, I've been wanting to get back into leatherworking. I used to make wallets and belts in my 30s but haven't touched it in years. My dad's 82 and has arthritis, so I've been helping him with more physical tasks around the house.
Leatherworking is such a valuable skill! It's also something you could potentially teach your dad if his hands allow it. What kind of projects are you thinking about?

I'd love to make a custom leather portfolio for work - something to carry my laptop and documents when I meet with clients. I'm also considering learning DJing software. I went to an underground music festival last summer and was mesmerized by the DJ's setup.
Those are two very different skills! The portfolio would be practical for work, while DJing sounds like a fun creative outlet. Have you looked into any courses or resources for either?

I found a leatherworking workshop at the Oregon College of Art next month. For DJing, I've been watching YouTube tutorials on Ableton Live. I'm thinking of buying some second-hand equipment from a guy on Craigslist.

Sounds like you've got a solid plan! The workshop will give you hands-on experience, and YouTube is great for DJing basics. Just be careful with Craigslist purchases - maybe test the equipment before buying?
